talk_title: Role of Sex and Ancestry in Inflammaging, Lymphomagenesis and Its Precision Therapy

speaker.name: Ari Melnick
speaker.affiliation: Jose Carreras Leukemia Research Center, Barcelona

biomarkers.name: TLR7 expression
biomarkers.finding: higher in female BN2 DLBCLs and correlates with IRAK inhibitor sensitivity

biomarkers.name: X chromosome hypomethylation
biomarkers.finding: female BN2 DLBCLs show reduced X methylation and increased TLR7 expression

biomarkers.name: Senescence-associated GLB1 staining
biomarkers.finding: strongly increased in African ancestry DLBCLs and associated with worse survival

models.name: SPEN truncation + Notch2 gain-of-function
models.genotype_or_key_features: SPEN haploinsufficient, Notch2 truncation, female mice

models.name: DLBCL patient cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: ~4,500 sequenced patients, ancestry and sex annotations

clinical_data.population: BN2 DLBCL with SPEN+Notch2 double mutation

clinical_data.metric: hazard ratio for high vs low GDF15

MOA_summary: Sex- and ancestry-specific mutations (SPEN, Notch2, ATM, MGA, SETD2) reprogram B-cell differentiation toward aged, auto-reactive AIBCs, driving aggressive DLBCL phenotypes; female BN2 DLBCLs exhibit X-chromosome instability and heightened TLR7 signaling, rendering them sensitive to IRAK inhibition, while African ancestry DLBCLs display senescence-associated secretory phenotypes linked to SETD2 loss, suggesting precision therapeutic windows.
key_findings: SPEN truncation co-occurs with Notch2 truncation in BN2 DLBCLs and predicts inferior survival in a 4,500-patient cohort.
Female BN2 DLBCL mice develop X‑chromosome hypomethylation, increased TLR7 expression, and are more sensitive to IRAK inhibitors than males.
African ancestry DLBCLs harbor frequent ATM, MGA, and SETD2 mutations (>50%) and show elevated senescence (GLB1) and exhausted CD4 T‑cell infiltrates.
SETD2‑heterozygous DLBCL cells are hypersensitive to a clinical SETD2 inhibitor, offering a potential ancestry‑specific therapy.
IRAK inhibition reduces tumor growth in female BN2 DLBCL xenografts, supporting a sex‑specific precision therapy approach.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Female BN2 DLBCLs display selective IRAK inhibitor sensitivity due to X‑linked TLR7 overexpression.
SETD2‑mutant African ancestry DLBCLs are resistant to standard therapies but vulnerable to SETD2 inhibition.

combination_strategies.rationale: Target TLR7‑driven signaling in female BN2 DLBCLs with X‑instability
combination_strategies.phenotype: significant tumor regression in female mice

combination_strategies.rationale: Exploit synthetic lethality in SETD2‑heterozygous African ancestry DLBCLs

open_questions: What molecular mechanisms link X‑chromosome hypomethylation to TLR7 overexpression in female BN2 DLBCLs?
Can GDF15 modulation improve outcomes in the described DLBCL subtypes?
Are there additional sex‑specific vulnerabilities beyond IRAK signaling?

talk_title: Age-Associated Molecular Landscapes and Therapeutic Responses in Breast Cancer

speaker.name: Sandra McAllister
speaker.affiliation: Brigham Hospital and Harvard Medical School

biomarkers.name: EMT gene signature
biomarkers.finding: enriched in older triple‑negative breast cancer tumors and mouse models

biomarkers.name: HLA‑E / A2Q1 expression
biomarkers.finding: higher in aged tumors, associated with immune exhaustion

models.name: ELEVATE study
models.genotype_or_key_features: women >70 years, longitudinal tissue and blood collection, n≈1500

models.name: NET1 transplantable TNBC model
models.genotype_or_key_features: young (8‑10 wk) vs aged (12‑18 mo) mice

models.name: NET1 barcoded clones
models.genotype_or_key_features: single‑cell derived populations with molecular barcodes

clinical_data.value: significant tumor regression in young mice, no significant benefit in aged mice
clinical_data.population: young vs aged mice with NET1 tumors

clinical_data.value: extended in aged mice with panobinostat + anti‑PD‑L1

MOA_summary: Aging reprograms breast cancer transcriptomes: older ER‑positive tumors show reduced immune and metabolic pathways, while older triple‑negative tumors acquire an inflamed, EMT‑driven phenotype; age‑selected subclones (e.g., barcode 25) become resistant to paclitaxel, but are sensitized to HDAC inhibition combined with checkpoint blockade, revealing age‑specific therapeutic vulnerabilities.
key_findings: Older breast cancer patients exhibit distinct molecular landscapes: ER+ tumors lose immune/metabolic activity, while TNBCs gain an inflamed EMT phenotype.
In mouse models, aged mice show slower NET1 tumor growth but reduced response to paclitaxel + anti‑PD‑L1, despite higher CD8 infiltration and Treg expansion.
Barcode 25 subclone is enriched by age and paclitaxel, displays intrinsic paclitaxel resistance, and can be eliminated by HDAC inhibition.
Panobinostat combined with anti‑PD‑L1 dramatically extends survival in aged mice, whereas the same combination is less effective in young mice without chemotherapy.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Barcode 25 subclone confers paclitaxel resistance and limits combination efficacy in aged mice.
HDAC inhibitors selectively target the age‑enriched resistant clone.

combination_strategies.phenotype: robust tumor regression in young mice, minimal benefit in aged mice
enhanced response in young mice when combined with chemo; limited effect in aged mice
significant survival benefit in aged mice when combined with anti‑PD‑L1

open_questions: What mechanisms drive the EMT and immune exhaustion programs uniquely in aged TNBC?
Can selective HDAC isoform inhibitors improve efficacy while reducing toxicity in older patients?
How does the middle‑aged cohort (40‑60 yr) molecularly differ from young and old groups?

talk_title: Targeting GDF15‑Mediated Immunosuppression to Overcome Age‑Driven Immunotherapy Resistance in Pancreatic Cancer

biomarkers.name: circulating GDF15 level
biomarkers.finding: higher in PDAC patients vs healthy controls and predicts poorer OS

biomarkers.name: CD8 T‑cell infiltration
biomarkers.finding: increased after GDF15 neutralization in aged mice

models.name: orthotopic KPC PDAC model
models.genotype_or_key_features: KRAS^G12D; TP53 loss, young (8‑10 wk) vs aged (12‑18 mo) C57BL/6

models.name: GVAX vaccine trial PDAC patients
models.genotype_or_key_features: GDF15 high vs low, survival analysis

clinical_data.population: aged KPC mice treated with anti‑GDF15
high GDF15 PDAC patients in GVAX trial
aged mice vaccinated with KRAS peptide + anti‑GDF15

MOA_summary: Aging elevates systemic and tumor‑derived GDF15, which suppresses CD8⁺ T‑cell infiltration and impairs vaccine‑induced neoantigen responses in PDAC; neutralizing GDF15 restores T‑cell priming and synergizes with KRAS peptide vaccination and checkpoint blockade to achieve tumor regression in aged mouse models.
key_findings: Aged mice develop larger orthotopic KPC tumors and exhibit markedly reduced CD8⁺ T‑cell infiltration compared with young mice.
Circulating GDF15 is low in young mice but rises sharply in aged mice and further increases after tumor implantation.
Genetic or antibody‑mediated GDF15 neutralization halves tumor size in aged mice and restores CD8⁺ T‑cell infiltration; effect is lost after T‑cell depletion.
GDF15 neutralization enhances KRAS mutant peptide vaccine‑induced IFN‑γ T‑cell responses in aged mice.
In a PDAC GVAX trial, high tumor GDF15 expression correlates with ~2‑fold worse overall survival, independent of age.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: GDF15‑mediated immunosuppression is age‑specific; young mice show minimal response to GDF15 blockade.
Tumor‑intrinsic KRAS vaccine efficacy is limited in aged hosts unless GDF15 is neutralized.

combination_strategies.rationale: release T‑cell inhibition in the GDF15‑high TME
combination_strategies.phenotype: modest tumor control alone; enhanced when combined with GDF15 neutralization
significant increase in IFN‑γ ELISpot responses and tumor regression when combined with GDF15 blockade

open_questions: What is the precise receptor and downstream signaling pathway by which GDF15 suppresses CD8⁺ T‑cell trafficking in PDAC?
Can GDF15 neutralization improve responses to standard chemotherapy or other immunotherapies in human PDAC trials?
Are there biomarkers beyond circulating GDF15 that predict benefit from GDF15‑targeted therapy?
